what_it_does: Declares a registry of built-but-unwired /api routes, mapping each route to a disposition tag (to_wire, to_build_ui, voice_owned, backend_only) and a short note explaining its intended consumer or rationale.
observations: This file is a promoted data artifact that replaced a hardcoded _ORPHAN_ROUTES dict in suite.py on 2026-06-08.
It contains 37 route entries across four disposition tags.
The largest group is to_build_ui (12 routes under /api/cognition/*) for an authoring UI.
to_wire routes (14 entries) are built backend endpoints waiting for a real FE consumer.
voice_owned routes (9 entries) are reserved for the voice session lane.
Several notes reference specific surfacing by an AST consumer-fix and prior false-wiring via comments, docstrings, or print strings.
The _doc explicitly frames this as the first step toward a persisted dispositioned finding-store / coherence substrate.
standouts: Explicit shift from hardcoded dict to declared data per a no-hardcoding rule.
Routes are classified by finish/defer disposition rather than just listed as orphans.
Multiple entries carry historical audit notes about how they were discovered (AST consumer-fix, false-wiring).
conventions: Follows a no-hardcoding rule by externalizing a formerly inline dictionary into a JSON data file.
Uses a route-keyed object with a uniform {tag, note} shape.
Tags form a closed vocabulary: to_wire | to_build_ui | voice_owned | backend_only.
Notes mix functional intent with provenance/audit context.
concerns: The file mixes design intent, implementation status, and historical narrative in the same note field, which may make machine parsing harder.
There is no schema version, last-updated timestamp, or owner field beyond the _doc provenance sentence.
Some notes claim a consumer is DESIGNED-not-built or legacy without linking to the design artifact, so the registry alone cannot verify truth.
A route not in this file that lacks a consumer would cause the reachability gate to fail, but the file does not define how the gate reads or validates it.
notes: A declared ledger that turns disconnected API routes into dispositioned findings so a reachability gate can decide whether each orphan is waiting for UI wiring, voice ownership, backend-only use, or a new disconnection.
questions: Which component reads this file and enforces the reachability gate?
How are new routes discovered and added here, and who maintains the registry?
What is the exact schema/validation for tags and notes?
Are the referenced design artifacts (StudioSeams, CognitionView, etc.) defined elsewhere?
How does the gate distinguish a legitimate backend_only route from an accidental orphan?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file catalogs built-but-unwired /api routes with disposition tags exactly as the _doc describes.
intention_signals: It is trying to make API route disconnectedness an explicit, auditable, machine-readable design finding rather than an implicit code smell, so the system can gate reachability and route unfinished work to the right lane (UI build, voice session, backend-only, or investigation).
novelty: It treats orphan routes as a coherence disposition catalogue with a closed vocabulary of finish/defer tags and provenance notes, generalizing toward a persisted finding-store rather than a simple TODO list.
contribution: Provides a structured, route-level inventory of backend endpoints and their intended consumers, which a fused system can use to track wiring gaps, scope UI build targets, and attribute voice-owned or internal-only surfaces.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON registry of built-but-unwired /api routes with disposition tags (to_wire, to_build_ui, voice_owned, backend_only) and explanatory notes, serving as a declared coherence catalogue for a reachability gate.
intention_for_embedding: To externalize and track the finish/defer disposition of disconnected API routes as auditable design findings.
